title: bambamR: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: bambamR R package end--end RNA-seq analysis. covers full pipeline raw FASTQ/BAM files quality control, alignment, read counting, normalization, differential expression analysis, publication-ready visualizations including onco plots, volcano plots, heatmaps, PCA, MA plots.

title: bambamR: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: Graceful degradation: Bioconductor dependencies optional. package operates minimal mode (CRAN-) full mode (Bioconductor). never breaks Bioconductor missing. Consistent interface: exported functions use bb_ prefix. plot functions return ggplot2 objects easy customization. Bundled example data: Every feature can explored without downloading external files installing Bioconductor.

title: bambamR: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: Install CRAN (available): Install development version GitHub: Optionally install Bioconductor packages full-mode features:
code: install.packages("bambamR") pak::pak("rabanheller/bambamR") BiocManager::install(c(   "DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma",   "ShortRead", "Rsamtools",   "GenomicAlignments", "GenomicRanges" )) library(bambamR)

code: de_results <- bb_example_de() sig <- sum(de_results$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE) cat("Significant genes (FDR < 0.05):", sig, "\n") #> Significant genes (FDR < 0.05): 20 bb_volcano(de_results, fc_cutoff = 1, p_cutoff = 0.05, n_label = 8) bb_volcano(de_results,            fc_cutoff = 0.5,            p_cutoff = 0.01,            label_genes = c("TP53", "KRAS", "EGFR", "PTEN"),            colors = c(up = "#E41A1C", down = "#377EB8", ns = "grey80"))

title: bambamR: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: Three DE methods supported, requiring optional Bioconductor package. three return standardized data.frame columns gene, log2fc, pvalue, padj, optionally basemean. standardized output ensures bb_volcano(), bb_ma_plot(), bb_heatmap(), bb_export_csv() work identically regardless DE method used.
code: # DESeq2 (requires DESeq2) de <- bb_deseq2(counts, metadata)  # edgeR (requires edgeR) de <- bb_edger(counts, group = metadata$condition)  # limma-voom (requires limma + edgeR) design <- model.matrix(~ condition, data = metadata) de <- bb_limma_voom(counts, design)

title: bambamR: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
code: bb_export_csv(de_results, "de_results.csv") bb_export_tsv(de_results, "de_results.tsv") bb_export_rds(de_results, "de_results.rds")

title: bambamR: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: bb_pipeline() function runs entire analysis end--end. accepts entry three stages: returned bb_result object bundles counts, metadata, DE results, generated plots:
code: # From FASTQ files result <- bb_pipeline(   fastq_dir    = "raw_reads/",   genome_index = "STAR_index/",   annotation   = "genes.gtf",   sample_info  = metadata,   aligner      = "STAR",   de_method    = "DESeq2",   threads      = 8 )  # From BAM files (skip alignment) result <- bb_pipeline(   bam_dir     = "aligned/",   annotation  = "genes.gtf",   sample_info = metadata )  # From a count matrix (skip alignment + counting) result <- bb_pipeline(   count_matrix = my_counts,   sample_info  = metadata,   de_method    = "edgeR" ) result$counts          # count matrix result$de_results      # standardized DE data.frame result$plots$pca       # ggplot object result$plots$volcano   # ggplot object result$plots$heatmap   # ggplot object

title: bambamR: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots

code: bb_run_app()

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: bb_oncoplot() function creates publication-ready onco plots (waterfall plots) showing mutation landscape across samples. built entirely ggplot2 returns modifiable ggplot object.
code: library(bambamR) #> bambamR: Full mode (all Bioconductor packages available) library(ggplot2)

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: bb_oncoplot() accepts two input formats:

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: data.frame columns sample, gene, mutation_type:
code: # Load bundled example mutation data ex <- bb_example_mutations() mut_data <- ex$mutations clinical_data <- ex$clinical head(mut_data) #>     sample   gene     mutation_type #> 1 TCGA-021   NRAS      In_Frame_Ins #> 2 TCGA-037 PIK3CA Missense_Mutation #> 3 TCGA-037   BRAF       Splice_Site #> 4 TCGA-031 PIK3CA   Frame_Shift_Ins #> 5 TCGA-042    RB1 Missense_Mutation #> 6 TCGA-008   NRAS Missense_Mutation

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: data.frame MAF-style columns: Hugo_Symbol, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Variant_Classification.

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
code: bb_oncoplot(mut_data, n_genes = 10)

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: Pass named character vector mutation_colors:
code: my_colors <- c(   "Missense_Mutation" = "#3182BD",   "Nonsense_Mutation" = "#E6550D",   "Frame_Shift_Del"   = "#31A354",   "Frame_Shift_Ins"   = "#756BB1",   "Splice_Site"       = "#DE2D26",   "In_Frame_Del"      = "#636363",   "Multi_Hit"         = "#FDAE6B",   "Other"             = "#BDBDBD" )  bb_oncoplot(mut_data, n_genes = 10, mutation_colors = my_colors)

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
code: bb_oncoplot(mut_data, genes = c("TP53", "KRAS", "PIK3CA", "BRAF", "EGFR"))

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: Provide data.frame sample annotations:
code: # Use the bundled clinical annotations bb_oncoplot(mut_data, n_genes = 8, annotation_df = clinical_data)

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: Sort samples mutation frequency (default) co-occurrence clustering:
code: bb_oncoplot(mut_data, n_genes = 8, sort_by = "cluster")

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
text: Since bb_oncoplot() returns ggplot object, can customize:
code: p <- bb_oncoplot(mut_data, n_genes = 8, show_barplot = FALSE,                   title = "Mutation Landscape") p + theme(legend.position = "right")

title: Creating Onco Plots with bambamR
code: p <- bb_oncoplot(mut_data, n_genes = 15, title = "Cohort Mutation Landscape") ggsave("oncoplot.pdf", p, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300) ggsave("oncoplot.png", p, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

title: bambamR Quick Start Guide
text: bambamR end--end RNA-seq analysis toolkit takes raw sequencing data (FASTQ/BAM) alignment, counting, normalization, differential expression, publication-ready visualizations. operates two modes: Minimal mode (CRAN-): CPM/TPM normalization, plots, FASTQ import via base R, system tool wrappers. Bioconductor needed. Full mode (+ Bioconductor): DESeq2/edgeR/limma DE analysis, TMM/RLE normalization, native BAM/FASTQ parsing via Rsamtools/ShortRead. vignette walks complete analysis using bundled example data – external files required.

title: bambamR Quick Start Guide
code: # CRAN install.packages("bambamR")  # GitHub development version pak::pak("rabanheller/bambamR")  # Optional Bioconductor packages for full mode BiocManager::install(c("DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma", "ShortRead", "Rsamtools")) library(bambamR) #> bambamR: Full mode (all Bioconductor packages available)

title: bambamR Quick Start Guide
text: bambamR ships three example datasets accessible via convenience functions. Nothing download – bundled package. Let’s inspect data: metadata columns condition (control vs. treatment), batch, sex, age. first 20 genes count matrix simulated differential expression conditions.
code: # RNA-seq count matrix: 200 genes x 10 samples (5 control, 5 treatment) ex <- bb_example_counts() counts   <- ex$counts metadata <- ex$metadata dim(counts) #> [1] 200  10 counts[1:6, 1:5] #>        Sample_01 Sample_02 Sample_03 Sample_04 Sample_05 #> TP53         525       533       485       515       519 #> KRAS         502       471       498       510       475 #> EGFR         479       504       488       514       473 #> BRAF         454       456       481       453       497 #> PIK3CA       470       469       506       483       532 #> PTEN         461       473       513       475       479 metadata #>           condition batch sex age #> Sample_01   control     A   M  45 #> Sample_02   control     B   F  52 #> Sample_03   control     A   M  38 #> Sample_04   control     B   F  61 #> Sample_05   control     A   M  55 #> Sample_06 treatment     B   F  47 #> Sample_07 treatment     A   M  59 #> Sample_08 treatment     B   F  42 #> Sample_09 treatment     A   M  50 #> Sample_10 treatment     B   F  44

title: bambamR Quick Start Guide
text: CPM normalization works everywhere – Bioconductor needed: TPM normalization requires gene lengths (also bundled): Bioconductor installed can also use "tmm" (edgeR) "rle" (DESeq2):
code: cpm <- bb_normalize(counts, method = "cpm") head(cpm[, 1:3]) #>        Sample_01 Sample_02 Sample_03 #> TP53    5286.849  5348.076  4852.766 #> KRAS    5055.235  4725.974  4982.840 #> EGFR    4823.621  5057.093  4882.783 #> BRAF    4571.866  4575.465  4812.743 #> PIK3CA  4732.989  4705.906  5062.886 #> PTEN    4642.357  4746.042  5132.926 gene_lengths <- readRDS(   system.file("extdata", "example_gene_lengths.rds", package = "bambamR") ) tpm <- bb_normalize(counts, method = "tpm", gene_lengths = gene_lengths$length) head(tpm[, 1:3]) #>        Sample_01 Sample_02 Sample_03 #> TP53    1663.711  1692.779  1537.197 #> KRAS    4554.768  4282.901  4519.196 #> EGFR    1089.000  1148.358  1109.638 #> BRAF    3353.235  3375.418  3553.223 #> PIK3CA  1120.653  1120.729  1206.683 #> PTEN    2325.557  2391.343  2588.289 tmm <- bb_normalize(counts, method = "tmm")

what: Option B: Use Pre-computed DE Results (no Bioconductor!)
title: bambamR Quick Start Guide
text: bambamR bundles realistic pre-computed DE result set can explore downstream plots without installing Bioconductor:
code:
de_results <- bb_example_de() head(de_results[order(de_results$padj), ]) #>     gene   log2fc       pvalue       padj   basemean #> 1   TP53 2.339649 0.0007574367 0.02349398  997.58686 #> 2   KRAS 2.722469 0.0003361832 0.02349398   90.62812 #> 3   EGFR 2.444921 0.0008361128 0.02349398   47.01289 #> 4   BRAF 1.623780 0.0005590749 0.02349398  179.27216 #> 5 PIK3CA 1.271091 0.0004387668 0.02349398   12.32381 #> 6   PTEN 2.006420 0.0006551589 0.02349398 9793.59126 cat("Total genes:", nrow(de_results), "
") #> Total genes: 500 cat("Significant (padj < 0.05):", sum(de_results$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "
") #> Significant (padj < 0.05): 20 cat("Up-regulated (log2FC > 1):",     sum(de_results$padj < 0.05 & de_results$log2fc > 1, na.rm = TRUE), "
") #> Up-regulated (log2FC > 1): 10 cat("Down-regulated (log2FC < -1):",     sum(de_results$padj < 0.05 & de_results$log2fc < -1, na.rm = TRUE), "
") #> Down-regulated (log2FC < -1): 10

what: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
title: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: End--end RNA-seq processing FASTQ publication-ready plots. bambamR provides streamlined toolkit RNA-seq analysis covering FASTQ/BAM import, quality control, alignment, read counting, normalization, differential expression analysis, publication-ready visualizations including onco plots, volcano plots, heatmaps, PCA.

title: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: bambamR never breaks Bioconductor missing. Every function needs optional package checks first either falls back gracefully gives clear install instruction.

title: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: Optionally install Bioconductor packages full-mode features:
code: # Install from CRAN (when available) install.packages("bambamR")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("rabanheller/bambamR") if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))   install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install(c(   "DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma",   "ShortRead", "Rsamtools",   "GenomicAlignments", "GenomicRanges" ))

title: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
text: bambamR ships ready--use example data can explore every feature without downloading external files installing Bioconductor: small FASTQ file (example_reads.fastq, 100 reads) gene-length table also included inst/extdata/.

title: End-to-End RNA-Seq Processing from FASTQ to Publication-Ready Plots
code: library(bambamR)  # ── 1. Load example data ─────────────────────────────────── ex <- bb_example_counts() str(ex) # List of 2 #  $ counts  : int [1:200, 1:10] ... #  $ metadata: data.frame (10 obs. of 4 variables)  # ── 2. Normalize ─────────────────────────────────────────── cpm <- bb_normalize(ex$counts, method = "cpm")  # ── 3. PCA ───────────────────────────────────────────────── bb_pca(cpm, ex$metadata, color_by = "condition")  # ── 4. Heatmap of top variable genes ────────────────────── bb_heatmap(cpm, n_genes = 30)  # ── 5. Differential expression (requires DESeq2) ────────── de <- bb_deseq2(ex$counts, ex$metadata) bb_volcano(de) bb_ma_plot(de)  # ... or use the pre-computed DE results (no Bioconductor!) ─ de <- bb_example_de() bb_volcano(de, n_label = 10) bb_ma_plot(de)  # ── 6. Heatmap of top DE genes ──────────────────────────── bb_heatmap(cpm, de_result = de, n_genes = 30)  # ── 7. Oncoplot ─────────────────────────────────────────── mut <- bb_example_mutations() bb_oncoplot(mut$mutations, n_genes = 15, annotation_df = mut$clinical)  # ── 8. Read a FASTQ file ───────────────────────────────── fq <- system.file("extdata", "example_reads.fastq", package = "bambamR") reads <- bb_read_fastq(fq, n = 5) reads  # ── 9. Export ───────────────────────────────────────────── bb_export_csv(de, "de_results.csv")

title: Normalize Count Matrix — bb_normalize
text: CPM (Counts Per Million): counts / library_size * 1e6. Pure base R. TPM (Transcripts Per Million): Normalizes gene length library size. Pure base R requires gene_lengths. TMM (Trimmed Mean M-values): Uses edgeR::calcNormFactors(). Requires edgeR package. RLE (Relative Log Expression): Uses DESeq2::estimateSizeFactors(). Requires DESeq2 package.

title: Create Publication-Ready Oncoplot — bb_oncoplot
text: Generates waterfall-style oncoplot showing mutation landscape across samples. Accepts either simple data.frame sample, gene, mutation_type columns MAF-format data.frame.

title: Create Publication-Ready Oncoplot — bb_oncoplot
code: bb_oncoplot(   data,   genes = NULL,   n_genes = 20L,   sort_by = c("frequency", "cluster"),   annotation_df = NULL,   mutation_colors = NULL,   show_pct = TRUE,   show_barplot = TRUE,   title = NULL,   border_color = "white" )

title: Create Publication-Ready Oncoplot — bb_oncoplot
text: data data.frame columns sample, gene, mutation_type. Alternatively, MAF-like data.frame Hugo_Symbol, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Variant_Classification columns. genes Character vector. Specific genes display. NULL, top n_genes frequently mutated genes shown. n_genes Integer. Number top mutated genes genes NULL. Default 20. sort_by Character. sort samples: "frequency" (mutation burden) "cluster" (co-occurrence clustering). Default "frequency". annotation_df data.frame sample annotations (bottom tracks). Rownames must sample identifiers. column becomes annotation track. mutation_colors Named character vector mapping mutation types colors. NULL, default nature-style palette used. show_pct Logical. Show mutation percentage per gene right margin. Default TRUE. show_barplot Logical. Show top (sample burden) side (gene count) barplots. Default TRUE. title Character NULL. Plot title. border_color Character NULL. Color tile borders. Default "white".

title: Create Publication-Ready Oncoplot — bb_oncoplot
text: ggplot2::ggplot object (composed patchwork barplots shown patchwork available).

title: Create Publication-Ready Oncoplot — bb_oncoplot
text: oncoplot encodes mutation landscape cohort tiled grid: columns = samples, rows = genes. Tiles colored mutation type. Multi-hit genes (multiple mutation types one sample) labeled "Multi_Hit". show_barplot = TRUE patchwork package available, plot composed three panels: top barplot (mutation burden per sample), main tile plot, right barplot (mutations per gene). Without patchwork, main tile plot returned.

title: Run Full bambamR Pipeline — bb_pipeline
text: fastq_dir Character NULL. Directory containing FASTQ files. provided, pipeline starts alignment. bam_dir Character NULL. Directory containing BAM files. provided, pipeline starts read counting. count_matrix numeric matrix NULL. Pre-computed count matrix. provided, pipeline starts normalization/DE. output_dir Character. Output directory results. Default "bambamR_output". genome_index Character NULL. Path genome index. Required starting FASTQ. annotation Character NULL. Path GTF annotation. Required starting FASTQ BAM. sample_info data.frame sample metadata. Must contain condition column (variable design). Rownames match sample identifiers. aligner Character. Aligner FASTQ alignment. Default "STAR". de_method Character. DE method: "DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma". Default "DESeq2". design formula DE analysis. Default ~ condition. skip Character vector. Steps skip: "qc", "align", "count", "de", "viz". Default character(0). threads Integer. Number threads. Default 4.

title: bambamR 0.1.0
text: Initial release bambamR. Data import: bb_read_fastq() bb_read_bam() ShortRead/Rsamtools base-R/system-samtools fallback. Quality control: bb_qc(), bb_qc_summary(), bb_plot_qc() per-read quality, GC content, read-length distributions. Alignment: bb_align() wrapping STAR, HISAT2, minimap2. Read counting: bb_count_reads() via GenomicAlignments featureCounts. Normalization: bb_normalize() supporting CPM, TPM (base R), TMM (edgeR), RLE (DESeq2). Differential expression: bb_deseq2(), bb_edger(), bb_limma_voom() returning standardized result data.frames. Visualization: bb_oncoplot(), bb_volcano(), bb_heatmap(), bb_pca(), bb_ma_plot() — return ggplot2 objects. Pipeline orchestrator: bb_pipeline() runs full analysis FASTQ, BAM, count matrix plots single call. Export: bb_export_csv(), bb_export_tsv(), bb_export_rds(). Shiny app: bb_run_app() launches interactive analysis dashboard. Example data: bb_example_counts(), bb_example_mutations(), bb_example_de() provide bundled datasets exploring features. Two operating modes: minimal (CRAN-) full (+ Bioconductor). Bioconductor dependencies optional checked runtime.
